column6: Activates rear delts/rhomboids to set scapula.
Wide grip; pass the band overhead and behind in one smooth arc. Opens the shoulders through full range before any pressing.
column7: Widen the grip if it pinches — never force the pass-over.

column6: Rotator cuff activation: elbow pinned to the side at 90 degrees; rotate the hand out slowly and control the return.
Isometric external-rotation hold at 90/90 against light cable tension. Builds cuff tendon resilience and primes shoulder stability under load.
column7: Light weight — a steady hold with no shaking.

column6: Hands behind head with the mid-back on the bench edge; extend over it to mobilize the upper back vertebrae.
Upper pec focus. 15-30 degree incline. The Smith bar path makes heavy solo pressing safe.
Core/Glute stability required. Do not arch back. Unrack from the J-hooks at shoulder height.
Chest and triceps compound replacing dips. Hands just inside shoulder width; tuck elbows ~45 degrees.
column7: No spotter needed — rotate the Smith bar to rack it anytime.

column6: Step into a staggered stance and let the hands travel behind the chest plane for a deep pec stretch before sweeping up. Last set: stretched-position partials after failure.
Unilateral delt work with tension in the stretched bottom position dumbbells can't give. Lean away holding an upright. Last set: bottom-half partials after failure.
Primary triceps move — the overhead stretch grows the long head ~1.4x more than pushdowns. Last set: stretched-position partials after failure.
Single-arm extension pulling across the body. Constant tension through the full range with a hard lockout squeeze.
External rotation for rotator cuff health.
Anti-extension: kneel and roll the bar out only as far as you can keep ribs down.
column7: Stop before the lower back arches.

column6: Hip & trunk flexion: control the descent; no swinging.
Metabolic finisher: quick alternating step-ups; full foot on the step.
